meta.name: WHO-5 Well-Being Index
meta.description: The WHO-5 is a short, self-reported measure of current mental well-being developed by the World Health Organization. It consists of 5 positively worded items assessing subjective well-being over the past two weeks. It can also be used as a screening tool for depression, where low scores indicate a need for further evaluation.
meta.author: WHO (1998)
meta.reference: Topp CW, Østergaard SD, Søndergaard S, Bech P. The WHO-5 Well-Being Index: a systematic review of the literature. Psychother Psychosom. 2015;84(3):167-176.
meta.instruction: Please indicate for each of the five statements which is closest to how you have been feeling over the last two weeks.

commonChoices.label: Less than half of the time
More than half of the time

questions.text: I have felt cheerful and in good spirits
I have felt calm and relaxed
I have felt active and vigorous
I woke up feeling fresh and rested
My daily life has been filled with things that interest me

ranges.label: Poor Well-Being
ranges.description: Your well-being score is very low, which may indicate significant emotional distress or possible depression. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider consulting a healthcare professional for a depression screening
Reach out to a trusted friend, family member, or counselor
Try to engage in activities that previously brought you joy
Prioritize basic self-care: sleep, nutrition, and gentle movement

ranges.label: Low Well-Being
ranges.description: Your well-being score is below average. You may be experiencing reduced positive emotions and energy.
suggestions: Consider a more detailed mental health assessment
Increase engagement in pleasurable and meaningful activities
Maintain social connections and share your feelings with others

ranges.label: Moderate Well-Being
ranges.description: Your well-being is at a moderate level. There is room for improvement in your overall sense of well-being.
suggestions: Continue to build positive daily habits
Ensure adequate sleep and physical activity
Engage in social activities and maintain supportive relationships

ranges.label: High Well-Being
ranges.description: Your well-being score is high. You are experiencing good levels of positive emotions, energy, and engagement with life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle habits
Share your well-being strategies with others
Stay engaged with activities that bring you fulfillment
Practice resilience-building to sustain your well-being
